message:
chore: wire cancellation e2e + close ft-e34d9.10.3.2

Wire ft_e34d9_10_3_2_cancellation into scripts/e2e_test.sh
(scenario registration + runner function + dispatch case).
Close ft-e34d9.10.3.2 (26 unit + 21 proptest = 47 tests pass).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -3221,7 +3221,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-e34d9.10.2.4","title":"[asupersync][substrate] User-facing latency/throughput contract and regression gates","description":"Define and enforce a user-facing performance contract for runtime migration so correctness improvements do not degrade responsiveness. Scope includes human CLI and robot-mode operations plus startup/shutdown and long-running watch flows.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement the full scope described in this bead Objective/Details/Goals with no functional regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge/boundary conditions, and explicit error/failure paths for the touched logic.
3. Add integration/E2E scenario scripts that validate real workflow behavior end-to-end, including at least one failure-injection path and one recovery path.
4. Ensure E2E scenarios emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, input, decision/outcome, reason_code/error_code) and retain artifacts for triage.
5. Validate determinism and policy/safety constraints for this change, including repeat-run stability and contract-level assertions on touched interfaces.
6. Wire new/updated tests into the project test workflow and document exact execution commands/log locations in implementation notes.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Establish baseline and post-migration benchmarks for ft robot state/get-text/send/wait-for/search/events, ft watch startup+steady-state, ft status, and snapshot/session listing workflows.\n2. Define p50/p95/p99 latency, throughput, and startup-time SLO thresholds with explicit pass/fail criteria and rationale.\n3. Unit tests validate benchmark helper math, threshold evaluation logic, and metric normalization.\n4. Integration tests replay representative mixed workloads across core+vendored paths and assert no user-visible regressions.\n5. E2E performance scripts emit detailed structured logs (run_id, scenario_id, seed, operation, latency_ms, queue_depth, error_class) and artifact manifests.\n6. Heavy benchmark and stress runs are executed via rch exec -- ... with reproducible command evidence.","notes":"This bead turns user experience into a first-class migration gate. It makes responsiveness and regression risk explicit before cutover. Include explicit rollback/fallback/recovery validation criteria with pass/fail outcomes for user-facing workflows. All diagnostics/logging/artifact outputs in this bead must comply with secret-safe redaction and privacy policy gates defined in ft-e34d9.10.7.5, with evidence captured in validation artifacts.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:46:57.717333Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-22T23:43:36.070075Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","e2e-tests","integration-tests","latency","logging","performance","quality-gate","sequencing","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.2.4","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.2.4","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.2.4","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.2.4","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-e34d9.10.3","title":"[asupersync][track] Structured concurrency and cancellation correctness","description":"Make structured concurrency and cancel-correctness first-class behavior, not optional conventions.

Scope:
- scoped task tree for long-lived services
- two-phase shutdown semantics
- cancellation-safe channel/message pathways

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement the full scope described in this bead Objective/Details/Goals with no functional regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge/boundary conditions, and explicit error/failure paths for the touched logic.
3. Add integration/E2E scenario scripts that validate real workflow behavior end-to-end, including at least one failure-injection path and one recovery path.
4. Ensure E2E scenarios emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, input, decision/outcome, reason_code/error_code) and retain artifacts for triage.
5. Validate determinism and policy/safety constraints for this change, including repeat-run stability and contract-level assertions on touched interfaces.
6. Wire new/updated tests into the project test workflow and document exact execution commands/log locations in implementation notes.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. All long-lived async services execute under explicit scope hierarchy with clear ownership.\n2. Shutdown follows request->drain->finalize semantics with checkpointed cancellation.\n3. Critical message flows use cancellation-safe patterns (reserve/commit or equivalent guarantees).\n4. Add deterministic unit/integration tests for cancellation races, shutdown idempotence, and message durability.\n5. Add e2e failure-injection scenarios with structured cancellation telemetry.\n6. Prove user/session continuity: no silent loss of command acknowledgements, control events, or critical state transitions in human and robot workflows.\n7. Heavy stress and failure-matrix runs are executed via rch exec -- ... with preserved logs.","notes":"This is the semantic heart of asupersync adoption. Success is measured by predictable failure behavior and recoverable user experience, not compile-green status. Include explicit rollback/fallback/recovery validation criteria with pass/fail outcomes for user-facing workflows. Require structured artifact/evidence manifests (logs, traces, command records, outputs) in closure evidence. This bead must include comprehensive unit tests for scope-local logic, edge cases, and failure branches with deterministic fixtures and replayable seeds. All diagnostics/logging/artifact outputs in this bead must comply with secret-safe redaction and privacy policy gates defined in ft-e34d9.10.7.5, with evidence captured in validation artifacts.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:24:36.274012Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-22T23:42:39.011623Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","cancellation","concurrency","e2e-tests","integration-tests","logging","quality-gate","sequencing","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.3","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.3","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.3","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-e34d9.10.3.1","title":"[asupersync][concurrency] Scope tree for daemons, watchers, workers","description":"Model and implement explicit scope hierarchy for every long-lived task domain (watch daemon, IPC workers, web server tasks, background indexing).

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement the full scope described in this bead Objective/Details/Goals with no functional regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge/boundary conditions, and explicit error/failure paths for the touched logic.
3. Add integration/E2E scenario scripts that validate real workflow behavior end-to-end, including at least one failure-injection path and one recovery path.
4. Ensure E2E scenarios emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, input, decision/outcome, reason_code/error_code) and retain artifacts for triage.
5. Validate determinism and policy/safety constraints for this change, including repeat-run stability and contract-level assertions on touched interfaces.
6. Wire new/updated tests into the project test workflow and document exact execution commands/log locations in implementation notes.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Each long-lived task has parent scope ownership and defined shutdown contract.\n2. No orphan spawn patterns remain in targeted domains.\n3. Unit tests validate scope lifecycle rules and parent-child cancellation propagation.\n4. Integration tests cover multi-scope startup/shutdown sequencing.\n5. E2E failure scenarios confirm child tasks terminate predictably with structured logs.","notes":"Scope topology is the enforceable architecture of concurrency. Keep topology diagrams and tests in sync. Include explicit rollback/fallback/recovery validation criteria with pass/fail outcomes for user-facing workflows. Require structured artifact/evidence manifests (logs, traces, command records, outputs) in closure evidence. Require deterministic fixtures/seeds and replayable test evidence so failures are reproducible across environments. All diagnostics/logging/artifact outputs in this bead must comply with secret-safe redaction and privacy policy gates defined in ft-e34d9.10.7.5, with evidence captured in validation artifacts. Heavy compile/test/benchmark/soak workloads for this bead must run via rch exec -- <command>, with reproducible command records and artifact paths captured in completion evidence.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:26:13.962304Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-25T08:15:50.075317Z","closed_at":"2026-02-25T08:15:50.075294Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","concurrency","e2e-tests","integration-tests","logging","quality-gate","scope","sequencing","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.3.1","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.3.1","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.3.1","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1513,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.3.1","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Validation: scope_tree.rs 1317 lines, 18 unit tests, 14/14 proptests, 11/11 e2e steps. Commit c666999c.","created_at":"2026-02-25T08:15:45Z"}]}
-{"id":"ft-e34d9.10.3.2","title":"[asupersync][concurrency] Two-phase cancellation + shutdown protocol rollout","description":"Adopt request->drain->finalize shutdown protocol across async services, replacing implicit drop-based cancellation assumptions.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement the full scope described in this bead Objective/Details/Goals with no functional regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge/boundary conditions, and explicit error/failure paths for the touched logic.
3. Add integration/E2E scenario scripts that validate real workflow behavior end-to-end, including at least one failure-injection path and one recovery path.
4. Ensure E2E scenarios emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, input, decision/outcome, reason_code/error_code) and retain artifacts for triage.
5. Validate determinism and policy/safety constraints for this change, including repeat-run stability and contract-level assertions on touched interfaces.
6. Wire new/updated tests into the project test workflow and document exact execution commands/log locations in implementation notes.

3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Shutdown protocol implemented and documented for all targeted service loops.\n2. Cancellation checkpoints are explicit at safe boundaries.\n3. Unit tests cover normal shutdown, repeated shutdown, and interrupted drain paths.\n4. Integration tests verify clean teardown under concurrent load.\n5. E2E scripts inject cancellations and assert no leaked tasks/resources with structured logs.\n6. User-facing interruption scenarios (Ctrl+C, pane shutdown, daemon restart, robot wait cancellation) are explicitly tested with deterministic outcomes.\n7. Heavy stress/cancellation suites run via rch exec -- ... with command evidence.","notes":"This is a behavioral contract, not just API migration. Ensure every service documents its shutdown state machine and user-visible outcomes. Include explicit rollback/fallback/recovery validation criteria with pass/fail outcomes for user-facing workflows. All diagnostics/logging/artifact outputs in this bead must comply with secret-safe redaction and privacy policy gates defined in ft-e34d9.10.7.5, with evidence captured in validation artifacts.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:26:14.601509Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-25T08:28:42.884316Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","cancellation","e2e-tests","integration-tests","logging","quality-gate","sequencing","shutdown","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.3.2","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.3.2","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.3.2","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"ft-e34d9.10.3.3","title":"[asupersync][concurrency] Cancellation-safe channel reserve/commit adoption","description":"Migrate high-value message pathways to cancellation-safe reserve/commit semantics to prevent silent message loss under cancellation.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement the full scope described in this bead Objective/Details/Goals with no functional regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge/boundary conditions, and explicit error/failure paths for the touched logic.
3. Add integration/E2E scenario scripts that validate real workflow behavior end-to-end, including at least one failure-injection path and one recovery path.
4. Ensure E2E scenarios emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, input, decision/outcome, reason_code/error_code) and retain artifacts for triage.
5. Validate determinism and policy/safety constraints for this change, including repeat-run stability and contract-level assertions on touched interfaces.
6. Wire new/updated tests into the project test workflow and document exact execution commands/log locations in implementation notes.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Critical channel flows identified and migrated to reserve/commit or equivalent safe semantics.\n2. Message durability guarantees documented per flow, with explicit user-impact classification (control plane, session state, telemetry).\n3. Unit tests simulate cancellation at each phase and verify delivery invariants.\n4. Integration tests validate producer/consumer behavior under backpressure and cancellation storms.\n5. E2E scenarios assert no message-loss regressions; structured logs include queue and commit state.\n6. Robot command/event workflows and operator alert pathways are included in mandatory no-loss scenarios.","notes":"Prioritize control-plane and safety-relevant flows first. Preserve causal traceability across retries/replays so users can trust automation outcomes. Heavy compile/test/benchmark/soak workloads for this bead must run via rch exec -- <command>, with reproducible command records and artifact paths captured in completion evidence. Include explicit rollback/fallback/recovery validation criteria with pass/fail outcomes for user-facing workflows. All diagnostics/logging/artifact outputs in this bead must comply with secret-safe redaction and privacy policy gates defined in ft-e34d9.10.7.5, with evidence captured in validation artifacts.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:26:15.363387Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-22T23:42:31.183608Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","channels","e2e-tests","integration-tests","logging","quality-gate","reliability","sequencing","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.3.3","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.3.3","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-e34d9.10.3.4","title":"[asupersync][concurrency] Orphan-task, deadlock, and stuck-cancel detectors","description":"Introduce detection tooling for orphan tasks, deadlock risks, and cancellation stalls during development and CI.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement the full scope described in this bead Objective/Details/Goals with no functional regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge/boundary conditions, and explicit error/failure paths for the touched logic.
3. Add integration/E2E scenario scripts that validate real workflow behavior end-to-end, including at least one failure-injection path and one recovery path.
4. Ensure E2E scenarios emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, input, decision/outcome, reason_code/error_code) and retain artifacts for triage.
5. Validate determinism and policy/safety constraints for this change, including repeat-run stability and contract-level assertions on touched interfaces.
6. Wire new/updated tests into the project test workflow and document exact execution commands/log locations in implementation notes.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Add runtime detectors and assertions for orphan/stuck tasks in test and debug modes.\n2. Deadlock-risk heuristics and lock-order checks are documented and instrumented.\n3. Unit tests validate detector signal correctness and low false-positive rate.\n4. Integration tests exercise known failure patterns and verify detector output.\n5. E2E scripts include detector telemetry in artifacts and fail on critical findings.\n6. Detector outputs must include actionable remediation hints for both human operators and robot workflows.","notes":"Detection closes the loop between doctrine and reality. Keep detectors cheap enough for routine CI use and make their output actionable, not merely descriptive. Heavy compile/test/benchmark/soak workloads for this bead must run via rch exec -- <command>, with reproducible command records and artifact paths captured in completion evidence. Include explicit rollback/fallback/recovery validation criteria with pass/fail outcomes for user-facing workflows. Require deterministic fixtures/seeds and replayable test evidence so failures are reproducible across environments. All diagnostics/logging/artifact outputs in this bead must comply with secret-safe redaction and privacy policy gates defined in ft-e34d9.10.7.5, with evidence captured in validation artifacts.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:26:16.147390Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-22T23:42:30.996974Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","diagnostics","e2e-tests","integration-tests","logging","quality-gate","reliability","sequencing","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.3.4","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.3.4","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.3.4","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-e34d9.10.4","title":"[asupersync][track] IPC and network stack harmonization","description":"Complete migration of IPC/networking surfaces so I/O semantics align with asupersync runtime guarantees.

Scope:
- Unix IPC and socket pathways
- TCP/TLS/HTTP surfaces
- timeout/backoff/error taxonomy harmonization

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement the full scope described in this bead Objective/Details/Goals with no functional regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge/boundary conditions, and explicit error/failure paths for the touched logic.
3. Add integration/E2E scenario scripts that validate real workflow behavior end-to-end, including at least one failure-injection path and one recovery path.
4. Ensure E2E scenarios emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, input, decision/outcome, reason_code/error_code) and retain artifacts for triage.
5. Validate determinism and policy/safety constraints for this change, including repeat-run stability and contract-level assertions on touched interfaces.
6. Wire new/updated tests into the project test workflow and document exact execution commands/log locations in implementation notes.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. IPC/socket/network paths are migrated to asupersync-compatible primitives without behavioral regressions.
2. Timeout/retry/backoff semantics are consistent across command, daemon, and service paths.
3. Error mapping is explicit and stable for operators and robot consumers.
4. Add unit/integration tests for connect/read/write/timeout/failure branches.
5. Add e2e network degradation scripts with detailed structured logs and artifacts.","notes":"Networking is where runtime mismatches become production incidents. This track forces consistency in transport semantics and failure handling. +run_scenario_ft_e34d9_10_3_2_cancellation() {
+    local scenario_dir="$1"
+    local case_name="ft_e34d9_10_3_2_cancellation"
+    local script_path="$PROJECT_ROOT/tests/e2e/test_ft_e34d9_10_3_2_cancellation.sh"
+    local scenario_stdout="$scenario_dir/${case_name}.stdout.log"
+    local before_snapshot="$scenario_dir/${case_name}.logs.before.txt"
+    local after_snapshot="$scenario_dir/${case_name}.logs.after.txt"
+
+    ls -1 "$PROJECT_ROOT/tests/e2e/logs"/ft_e34d9_10_3_2_* 2>/dev/null | LC_ALL=C sort >"$before_snapshot" || true
+
+    log_info "[$case_name] Step 1: running two-phase cancellation/shutdown e2e harness"
+    set +e
+    timeout "$TIMEOUT" bash "$script_path" >"$scenario_stdout" 2>&1
+    local rc=$?
+    set -e
+
+    if [[ "$rc" -eq 124 ]]; then
+        log_fail "[$case_name] harness timed out after ${TIMEOUT}s"
+        tail -n 120 "$scenario_stdout" >&2 || true
+        return 4
+    fi
+    if [[ "$rc" -ne 0 ]]; then
+        log_fail "[$case_name] harness failed (exit=$rc)"
+        tail -n 120 "$scenario_stdout" >&2 || true
+        return "$rc"
+    fi
+
+    ls -1 "$PROJECT_ROOT/tests/e2e/logs"/ft_e34d9_10_3_2_* 2>/dev/null | LC_ALL=C sort >"$after_snapshot" || true
+    while IFS= read -r log_path; do
+        if [[ -z "$log_path" ]]; then
+            continue
+        fi
+        if [[ ! -f "$before_snapshot" ]] || ! grep -Fxq "$log_path" "$before_snapshot"; then
+            cp -f "$log_path" "$scenario_dir/" || true
+        fi
+    done < "$after_snapshot"
+
+    log_pass "[$case_name] two-phase cancellation/shutdown e2e completed"
+    return 0
+}
+
 run_scenario_ft_e34d9_10_1_2_doctrine_pack() {
     local scenario_dir="$1"

+            ;;

             ;;
